name: American Gold Exchange & Pawn
description_short: Family-owned pawn shop in Raleigh, NC since 1994 offering quick cash loans, precious metal buying, and retail sales with no middlemen and A+ BBB rating.
description_long:
American Gold Exchange and Pawn, Inc. was established in 1994 and operates as a family-owned and operated pawnshop serving the Raleigh-Durham area, including Cary, Rocky Mount, Clayton, Garner, Knightdale, and Zebulon. The business has maintained continuity for over 20 years and emphasizes confidential, friendly service as a core operational principle.

The company operates as a full-service pawnshop with three primary business functions: (1) pawn loans where customers receive cash against collateral items, (2) retail sales of used and new merchandise at below-retail prices with 30-day warranties, and (3) cash purchases of items outright. They buy and loan on jewelry, televisions, game systems, tools, DVDs, and musical instruments. The company holds specialized licensing as a precious metals buyer and explicitly states they operate without middlemen, claiming this allows them to "PAY YOU MORE" for items.

Key distinguishing factors include their A+ Better Business Bureau rating, location-specific focus as a local shop rather than a chain, and their emphasis on honest appraisals and confidentiality. They offer additional services including jewelry repair, custom jewelry work, computer repair, and layaways. Their website emphasizes fair market-driven appraisals, fast transactions, and a family-friendly environment. Inventory is described as constantly rotating with daily changes.

Limitations evident from the website include inconsistency in phone numbers listed (both 984-231-9993 and 919-231-6433 appear), which may indicate outdated information. The website also contains unrelated content about deck restoration from "Luna Restoration," suggesting possible website maintenance issues or content management problems. No specific details are provided about loan terms, interest rates, repayment periods, or typical loan amounts, which are critical factors for consumers evaluating pawn loan options.
pros: A+ Better Business Bureau rating demonstrates established credibility and complaint resolution history
Over 20 years of continuous family ownership suggests stability and long-term community presence
Holds specialized precious metals buyer license and operates without middlemen, potentially enabling higher payouts
Offers multiple services beyond pawning: jewelry repair, custom jewelry, computer repair, and layaways
30-day warranty on retail merchandise provides buyer protection on purchases
Claims fast, confidential service with no credit checks required for pawn loans
Wide geographic service area covering Raleigh-Durham region with convenient Raleigh location
cons: Website lists two different phone numbers (984-231-9993 and 919-231-6433), creating confusion and potential contact issues
No published information on loan terms, APR, interest rates, or repayment period lengths, making it difficult for consumers to evaluate costs
Website contains unrelated content about deck restoration, indicating poor website maintenance and potential credibility concerns
No information about fees, late payment penalties, or conditions for loan default and item forfeiture
No transparency regarding appraisal methodology or how prices are determined for buyouts versus loans
best_for: Customers needing immediate cash who own valuable items and prefer to avoid credit checks
Local Raleigh residents seeking to buy used merchandise at discounted prices with warranty protection
Precious metals and jewelry sellers looking for specialized buyers without middleman markups
Consumers seeking jewelry repair and custom jewelry services alongside pawn options
diagnosis: American Gold Exchange and Pawn is best for borrowers in the Raleigh area who need immediate cash and own collateral items, or for bargain hunters seeking discounted used goods. Primary caveat: the website lacks critical loan term disclosures (APR, fees, repayment periods), and inconsistent contact information plus unrelated website content raise questions about operational organization and information reliability.
services: Retail pawn sales (buy used and new merchandise)
